title: luzula campestris subsp. alpestris
intro: Luzula campestris subsp. alpestris, also known as Alpine Woodrush or Alpine Wood-rush, is a sub-species of the Luzula campestris plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is often found in mountainous regions and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Luzula campestris subsp. alpestris include its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
